synthetic_generation_rules: Start from a canonical policy fact with one atomic answer containing a single owner, threshold, or action.
Generate variants by applying synonym swaps, typo insertion, punctuation noise, and formatting noise while preserving intent.
Keep the answer style constrained to one short sentence so exact and semantic comparisons remain stable.
real_world_redaction_policy: Real-world cases are redacted composites based on prior support, billing, and incident-management evaluation work.
Product names, queue names, account identifiers, and internal team labels were generalized or replaced.
Answers preserve the decision boundary but remove any sensitive operational details.

cases.description: Paraphrase, typo, and punctuation noise should not change the returned SLA.
cases.expected_answer: Contractor password resets are completed within 4 business hours.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: What is the password reset SLA for contractors?

query_variants.label: paraphrase
query_variants.query: How quickly should a contractor get a password reset completed?

query_variants.label: typo
query_variants.query: what is the contraktor password reset sla?

query_variants.label: punctuation-noise
query_variants.query: Password reset SLA for contractors???

cases.description: Numeric thresholds should stay stable under light wording changes.
cases.expected_answer: A batch job is marked failed after 3 retries.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: How many retries happen before a batch job is marked failed?

query_variants.label: compact-ask
query_variants.query: Retries before batch failure?

cases.description: Routing questions should return the canonical queue name.
cases.expected_answer: Vendor onboarding tickets go to the access operations queue.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: Which queue handles vendor onboarding tickets?

query_variants.label: paraphrase
query_variants.query: Where should vendor onboarding requests be routed?

cases.description: Retention windows should remain exact under terse phrasing.
cases.expected_answer: Sandbox logs are retained for 7 days.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: What retention period applies to sandbox logs?

query_variants.label: short-form
query_variants.query: Sandbox log retention?

cases.description: Instruction order and distractor details may cause drift even though the answer should stay the same.
cases.expected_answer: Escalate a blocked refund request after 2 unsuccessful processor attempts.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: When should an agent escalate a blocked refund request?

query_variants.label: instruction-order
query_variants.query: Answer briefly. Before you answer, consider whether this is about refunds or chargebacks. When should an agent escalate a blocked refund request?

query_variants.label: distractor-detail
query_variants.query: A blocked refund request also mentions that the customer changed billing countries last month. When should the agent escalate it?

cases.description: Redacted billing ownership edge case drawn from prior eval work.
cases.expected_answer: Billing operations owns duplicate invoice issues after plan downgrades.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: A customer was billed twice after a plan downgrade. Which team owns that issue?

query_variants.label: paraphrase
query_variants.query: Who should handle a duplicate invoice created right after a downgrade?

cases.description: Merged-ticket lifecycle decision with a clear recreate versus reopen boundary.
cases.expected_answer: Tickets merged more than 30 days ago should be recreated, not reopened.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: A user wants to reopen a merged ticket that was closed over 30 days ago. What should support do?

query_variants.label: compact-ask
query_variants.query: Merged ticket older than 30 days: reopen or recreate?

cases.description: Privacy workflow should enforce verification before action.
cases.expected_answer: Request account verification before processing a PII deletion request.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: A PII deletion request arrives without an account identifier. What is the next step?

query_variants.label: paraphrase
query_variants.query: No account ID was included with a deletion request for personal data. What should happen first?

cases.description: Incident escalation should follow customer impact, not the current status-page color.
cases.expected_answer: Open a severity 2 incident when multiple tenants are impacted despite a green status page.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: The status page is green, but several tenants have confirmed errors. What should the on-call responder do?

query_variants.label: paraphrase
query_variants.query: Multiple tenants are failing even though the public status looks healthy. What incident action is required?

cases.description: Approval workflows should reject incomplete exception requests.
cases.expected_answer: Reject the security exception request until an expiration date is provided.

query_variants.label: baseline
query_variants.query: A security exception request has no expiration date. What should the reviewer do?

query_variants.label: paraphrase
query_variants.query: The request asks for a security exception but leaves out the expiry. What is the correct decision?
